definition: Energetic persistence applied to practice and the development of wholesome qualities.
notes: The project prefers energy for viriya so it remains distinct from sammā-vāyāma, which is already rendered right effort. Vigor and persistence remain acceptable when a passage leans more toward forcefulness or steadiness than sheer energetic application. Ven. Dr. M. Punnaji sometimes uses strength in awakening-factor exposition; the repository records that as a source-backed alternate while retaining energy as the house default. The OSF glossary uses effort and persistent effort language for viriya, which the repository records as source-backed explanatory vocabulary alongside its current default.

context_rules.context: In most practical and awakening-factor contexts
In prose emphasizing robust force or strength of application
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the line wants more intensity.

context_rules.context: In prose emphasizing sustained steadiness over time
context_rules.notes: Use when the durational aspect matters most.

context_rules.context: In Punnaji-style pedagogical exposition of the awakening sequence
context_rules.notes: Source-backed alternate, not the house default.

example_phrases.notes: Frequent practical expression of active application.
Shows the term in the awakening-factor framework.
